Petition For Order Determining That The Amendment And Restatement Of Trust Is Invalid, Breach Of Contract, To Determine The Validity Of Provisions Of
Off Calendar Jan-26-2021 Continued To Feb-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Joseph Dermer appeared by zoom for Bonnie Roberts, James Ledger and Lee Ledger (Petitioners).
Jamay Lee appeared by zoom for Jacob Murphy (Trustee) and Dynah Murphy (Beneficiary).

Mr. Dermer stated that the parties have reached a settlement in the matter, that a settlement agreement has been drafted and circulated, that the respondents have signed it and that he is in the process of having his Clients sign it. Mr. Dermer also stated that one of his Clients in in Arkansas and that it may take five business days to get a signature. Ms. Lee questions if a dismissal is appropriate in probate. The Court informed Counsel to file the dismissal and that they should then contact the assigned Probate Examiner to request that the petition be taken off calendar. The Petition for Order Determining that the Amendment and Restatement of Trust is Invalid. etc., and status of ADR are continued to February 23, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Dermer is to provide a copy of the dismissal to the Probate Examiner.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Adr
Off Calendar Jan-26-2021 Continued To Feb-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Determining That The Amendment And Restatement Of Trust Is Invalid, Breach Of Contract, To Determine The Validity Of Provisions Of
Off Calendar Dec-08-2020 Continued To Jan-26-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Dermer appeared by telephone for Petitioners.
Jamay Lee appeared by zoom for and with Jake Murphy by zoom and Dynah Murphy by zoom.

The Court thanked the parties stating that it received the joint trial plan, that it was very helpful and that it seems like the parties are interested in mediation. Ms. Lee stated that they reached out to a Mediator and that they have mediation scheduled for January 11th. The Court stated that in light of that and the stay at home order, trials are not happening, and the matter will have to be continued. The Court also informed Counsel that this is an informal trial date and the date that the Court decides if the matter will remain in Probate, or be sent out for trial. The Petition for Order Determining that the Amendment and Restatement of Trust is Invalid, etc., and status of ADR are continued to January 26, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A report on status of ADR is to be filed by January 19, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Adr
Off Calendar Dec-08-2020 Continued To Jan-26-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Determining That The Amendment And Restatement Of Trust Is Invalid, Breach Of Contract, To Determine The Validity Of Provisions Of
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Continued To Dec-08-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Dermer appeared by zoom for Petitioners.
Jamay Lee appeared by telephone for Jacob Murphy and Dynah Murphy (Respondents).

Mr. Dermer stated that Mr. Ledger's deposition was taken last Friday on Sep. 4, 2020, that he notified Ms. Lee that he intends to amend the complaint to add one cause of action for drafting error, that he asked Ms. Lee to stipulate to the amendment and that he was told she needed to discuss it with her Clients. The Court informed Ms. Lee that in the case management order leave to amend is liberally granted. Mr. Dermer stated that he did not check the boxes regarding ADR (Alternative Dispute Resolution) because the Respondents do not plan to engage in any ADR. The Court informed Ms. Lee that she will have to participate in ADR before trial. Ms. Lee stated that she will notify her Clients. The Court questions whether the matter qualifies for pro bono mediation. Mr. Dermer stated that he does not know what the assets are. Ms. Lee stated that she believes the matter does qualify for pro bono mediation. The Court set the trial on December 8, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and also requires that the parties participate in some sort of ADR. If the parties want to participate in pro bono mediation, they are to contact the Clerk. Status on ADR is also set for December 8, 2020. The Petition for Order Determining that the Amendment and Restatement of Trust is Invalid, etc. is continued to December 8, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for trial. Mr. Dermer is to monitor the Court's register of actions to download a copy of the order and serve it on all parties.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Determining That The Amendment And Restatement Of Trust Is Invalid, Breach Of Contract, To Determine The Validity Of Provisions Of
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Continued To Apr-28-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Dermer appeared by telephone for Petitioners.

The Court noted that nobody is in Court today and that Mr. Lee is not present. Mr. Dermer stated that he has not heard from Mr. Lee, that Mr. Lee has been participating in discovery and that they had been communicating. Mr. Dermer also stated that he dismissed some of his causes of action in order to narrow the case, and that he is prepared to go to mediation, or set the matter for trial. The Petition for Order Determining that the Amendment and Restatement of Trust is Invalid, etc. is continued to April 28, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to meet and confer, and are to file a joint status statement by April 21, 2020 as to where things stand.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
